item.title: Marie Antoinette-Era Fashion Plates
item.description: Also: Daniel Radcliffe stars in “Every Brilliant Thing,” Robert Plant sings roots folk in a cathedral, a soulful retrospective of Beuford Smith, and more.

item.title: China’s Shifting Relationship to the Countryside
item.description: Catherine Hyland’s images show what happened after the giant migration to the cities.
item.dc:creator: ByPeter Hessler

item.title: What Was Behind the T.S.A. Meltdown?
item.description: The present mess has roots in two entangled, defining White House projects:DOGEand the mind-bending expansion ofICE.
item.dc:creator: ByBenjamin Wallace-Wells

item.title: Trump’s War Hits theChaiwalas
item.description: Restrictions and attacks in the Strait of Hormuz have made fuel prices rocket. Just ask the roadside tea venders in New Delhi.
item.dc:creator: ByNathan Heller

item.title: Stephanie Hsu Does the Time Warp
item.description: The “Everything Everywhere All at Once” actress returns to Broadway, playing the Susan Sarandon role in “Rocky Horror,” at Studio 54.
item.dc:creator: ByRachel Syme

item.title: Signed, Sealed, Delivered—and Afloat
item.description: The city’s Blue Highways distribution program aims to decrease truck emissions and road congestion while delivering your Sephora package. Its solution? Boats.
item.dc:creator: ByHenry Alford

item.title: Reality-TV Deconstructor
item.description: The author of the book “Dream Facades,” about the architecture in reality-TV shows, gives a tour of notable New York locations—including Bethenny Frankel’s old apartment.
item.dc:creator: ByEmma Allen

item.title: How I Became a Filmmaker
item.description: After college, I joined an odd little utopia of movie nerds working out of an office on lower Broadway. Then the sustainability of the setup started to seem questionable.
item.dc:creator: ByLena Dunham

item.title: The Camps Promising to Turn You—or Your Son—Into an Alpha Male
item.description: At the Men of War Crucible, you bear crawl through rivers. At Warrior Week, you dig your own grave. At the Squire Program, your teen-ager can take part, too.
item.dc:creator: ByCharles Bethea

item.title: He Helped Stop Iran from Getting the Bomb
item.description: A former C.I.A. officer says that he recruited scientists as part of the United States’ effort to disrupt Iran’s nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: ByDavid D. Kirkpatrick

item.title: At Synagogues, Tensions Are Boiling Over
item.description: Disagreements about Gaza and Zionism have divided congregations.
item.dc:creator: ByEyal Press

item.title: If I Made Novelty T-Shirts
item.description: Ever wondered why “I’m with Stupid”? Let me explain.
item.dc:creator: ByJesse Eisenberg

item.title: “Enough for Now”
item.description: She flipped through the diary, looking for her name. Was she hoping not to find herself, or did a perverse part of her want to?
item.dc:creator: ByCassandra Neyenesch

item.title: How to Measure the Good Life
item.description: In a new book, the conservative pundit Arthur C. Brooks offers tips to “young strivers” on maximizing their daily meaning quotient.
item.dc:creator: ByBecca Rothfeld

item.title: The Ample Rewards of Ben Lerner’s Slender New Novel
item.description: In “Transcription,” a novel about memory and influence, an interview with an aging intellectual goes unrecorded. Or does it?
item.dc:creator: ByGiles Harvey

item.title: Briefly Noted
item.description: Short reviews of recent releases.

item.title: Kia Damon’s Audacious Florida Cooking
item.description: A young Black chef from Orlando conjures a distinctive image of her home state, beyond the loud luxury of Miami and the kitsch of the Keys.
item.dc:creator: ByHannah Goldfield

item.title: “Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat” and Age of the Prestige Prank Show
item.description: The series, returning for a second season, is the latest example of a new breed—one that relies on elaborate, full-immersion experiments rather than on one-off stunts.
item.dc:creator: ByInkoo Kang

item.title: The Unseen Work of One of Iran’s Greatest Filmmakers
item.description: For the director Mani Haghighi, his country’s rich cinematic tradition is a family affair.
item.dc:creator: ByRichard Brody

item.title: “The Great-Grandmothers”
item.description: “One paid the rent with a row of her teeth.”
item.dc:creator: BySandra Lim

item.title: Following Bashō’s Narrow Walk Into the Interior
item.description: “White water-filled spheres / Floating in a rock garden / Ah! Whose dream is this?”
item.dc:creator: ByBob Holman

item.title: Cartoon Caption Contest
item.description: Submit a caption, plus vote on other entries and finalists.

item.title: Cartoons from the Issue
item.description: Drawings from the April 6, 2026, magazine.

item.title: The Crossword: Monday, March 30, 2026
item.description: A challenging puzzle.
item.dc:creator: ByAnna Shechtman
